title: HTTP Services, Clients, Boundaries, Security, and Graceful Shutdown · Runtime Contract: Graceful Shutdown Readiness
summary: A small service accepts hostile input, calls a slow dependency, serves concurrent requests, and must shut down without losing work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go graceful shutdown readiness increment in a resilient secure HTTP service design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Coordinate signal handling, readiness withdrawal, server shutdown, in-flight deadlines, background-worker completion, and forced-exit evidence.
Map internal failures to stable external responses while retaining structured correlation, cause, latency, and privacy-safe logs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Graceful Shutdown Readiness · Theory Graceful Shutdown Readiness
steps.instruction:
theory case — Service request 4 has a hostile label, oversized body, slow dependency, duplicate header, and client cancellation during shutdown. Bound resources, validate and encode by sink, preserve stable responses, and drain owned work without leaking internals.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Graceful Shutdown Readiness contract for the supplied data.
